Performs required safety checks before prescribing: asks about medication allergies (including prior reactions to sleep aids), reviews current medications/substances (including alcohol, cannabis, OTC antihistamines, opioids, benzodiazepines), and screens for contraindications/precautions (pregnancy/breastfeeding, severe hepatic disease, untreated sleep apnea, significant respiratory disease, history of falls, severe depression/suicidality).

Assesses for common secondary contributors and red flags before committing to a plan: depression/anxiety symptoms, thyroid symptoms, pain, nocturia, restless legs symptoms, snoring/witnessed apneas, and stimulant/caffeine use.

Sets expectations: CBT-I benefits accrue over weeks; medication (if used) should be time-limited and at the lowest effective dose; discusses potential adverse effects (next-day drowsiness, dizziness) and when to stop/seek care.
Provides a follow-up timeframe (e.g., 2-4 weeks) and clear return precautions (worsening mood, suicidal thoughts, severe daytime sleepiness, near-miss accidents, concerning parasomnias).
